_comment: WC2026 Group K, MD3. Standings before: Colombia 6 (3-1 Uzbekistan, 1-0 DR Congo), Portugal 4 (1-1 DR Congo, 4-0 Uzbekistan), DR Congo 1, Uzbekistan 0. This is essentially a SEEDING game: both are through -- Colombia top with 6, and Portugal on 4 are safe even in defeat (a loss leaves them 3rd on 4 pts = a safe best third, cf. Ghana). Colombia need only a draw to win the group; Portugal must win to top it. MOTIVATION CAVEAT (Mexico/Norway-France lesson): both qualified, but do NOT apply a rotation discount on speculation -- trust the strength baseline. Portugal the higher-rated, quality side (90 vs 83; hammered Uzbekistan 4-0) and the slight favourite by class; Colombia efficient and organized (only needs the draw -> may sit and counter). Near-even with a Portugal lean. Home = Portugal, away = Colombia. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).
_thirds_context: Both teams are already qualified, so the thirds table is not at stake for them: Colombia (6 pts) go through as 1st or 2nd; Portugal (4 pts) are 1st/2nd with a result and, even losing, finish 3rd on 4 pts which is a safe best third on current numbers. So this game decides SEEDING (1st vs 2nd), not survival -- which is exactly why no desperation lift applies to either side; play the class baseline.

meta.stakes: Seeding game: both already qualified. Colombia (6 pts) win the group with a draw; Portugal (4 pts) must win to top it (and are a safe best third even if they lose). Portugal the quality favourite; Colombia organized and may counter.

home.name: Portugal
notes: Quality side (rating 90); beat Uzbekistan 4-0, drew DR Congo 1-1. attack 1.20.
Already through but must win to top the group; no rotation discount on speculation (Mexico lesson).

away.name: Colombia
notes: Efficient (3-1 Uzbekistan, 1-0 DR Congo), rating 83. attack 1.0.
Top of the group; only needs a draw -> may sit deeper and counter. No motivation tweak, just class.

_lineups._comment: Confirmed/projected XIs (RotoWire/Sports Mole/beIN, ~60 min pre-KO). The key signal CONFIRMS the no-rotation calibration: despite both being through, Roberto Martinez fields a STRONG Portugal (Ronaldo + Bruno Fernandes in a 4-2-3-1) chasing the top seed and a kinder knockout path -> no rotation discount, exactly the Mexico/Norway-France lesson. Colombia ALSO name a full-strength side (the James / Luis Diaz / Luis Suarez front three) rather than resting on the draw they need -> small upward nudge for Colombia (1.08 -> 1.12), which also tightens the fit to the market. Net: both confirmed strong, the seeding-game read holds, Portugal the class favourite with Colombia a live, full-strength counter. Lambdas essentially unchanged.
